guidances.given_in: FY25 year-end commentary (~25%+ growth communicated for FY26)
guidances.note: FY26 standalone revenue of INR5,903.8mn (~INR590cr), +30.3% YoY, exceeding the 'previously communicated top line growth of approximately 25% plus' management referenced on the Q4 FY26 concall (16 May 2026).

Management reiterated confidence in '25% plus top line trajectory, not just for FY27, but on a multiyear basis' — driven by ramp-up of 4 newly capitalized plants (GE, Siemens, Mitsubishi, Baker Hughes) and commissioning of remaining plants under construction.

Order book of ~INR6,500cr (net of FY26 deliveries, ~11-12x FY26 revenue) expected to be substantially converted to revenue over the next 5 years, per Vishnu Malpani (Whole-Time Director): 'over the next 5 years, we should be able to more or less consume INR6,500 crores.'
Aerospace & Defence segment crossed INR100cr revenue for the first time in FY26 (INR101cr, ~17.2% of FY26 revenue, +~25% YoY), per Vishnu Malpani.

4 of 8 planned dedicated OEM facilities completed as of Q4 FY26 (GE, Siemens, Mitsubishi, Baker Hughes); remaining facilities under construction/WIP targeted for completion within FY27, per Vishnu Malpani.

guidances.for_period: no new date given — timeline shifted
guidances.note: The Baker Hughes-linked Saudi Arabia facility opportunity remains active but its timeline has been shifted ('due to the war', per an analyst's framing that management did not dispute) and deprioritized versus ramping up current domestic plants, per Vishnu Malpani: 'the time lines have been shifted... that opportunity still lies.'

Azad describes itself as 'one of the only qualified partners in the country' producing nuclear turbine airfoils, supplying EDF's Arabelle Solutions (France); positions this as an open growth avenue 'whether in India or globally', per Vishnu Malpani.
